questions.q: In which year was the Telugu film Sankarabharanam released?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam was released in 1980 and became a landmark in Telugu cinema.

questions.q: Who wrote and directed the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam was written and directed by K. Viswanath.

questions.q: In which language was the film Sankarabharanam originally made?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam is a Telugu-language film directed by K. Viswanath.

questions.q: The 1980 film Sankarabharanam is built around which tradition of Indian classical music?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam centres on Carnatic classical music and the life of a Carnatic vocalist.

questions.q: Who composed the celebrated musical score for the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: The music of Sankarabharanam was composed by K. V. Mahadevan.

questions.q: The central character of the 1980 film Sankarabharanam is a person of which profession?
questions.explanation: The film centres on an ageing Carnatic vocalist named Sankara Sastry.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead role of the Carnatic vocalist Sankara Sastry in the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: J. V. Somayajulu played the lead role of Sankara Sastry in Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: The title Sankarabharanam of the 1980 film refers to what?
options: A famous temple in South India
questions.explanation: Shankarabharanam is a well-known raga in Carnatic music, and the film takes its name from it.

questions.q: What kind of recognition did the 1980 film Sankarabharanam receive from the Indian government's film awards?
options: It received no awards at all
It was only a regional commercial hit
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam won several National Film Awards, including for its music.

questions.q: The 1980 film Sankarabharanam is widely credited with reviving popular interest in what?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam is credited with reviving popular interest in Indian classical music.

questions.q: Which playback singer's voice is most associated with the songs of Sankara Sastry in the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam sang the male classical songs for Sankarabharanam, a role for which he won acclaim.

questions.q: Who played the female lead Tulasi in the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: Manju Bhargavi played Tulasi, the devoted dancer in Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: Which female playback singer rendered many of the classical compositions in the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: S. Janaki sang several of the classical numbers in Sankarabharanam, earning critical praise.

questions.q: How was the 1980 film Sankarabharanam received commercially, given its classical-music subject?
options: It flopped badly at the box office
It was never given a wide release
It only earned money decades later
questions.explanation: Despite its serious classical theme, Sankarabharanam was an unexpected commercial blockbuster.

questions.q: K. Viswanath, director of Sankarabharanam, became especially known for films centred on which themes?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was renowned for films celebrating Indian classical arts, dance and music, of which Sankarabharanam is the most famous.

questions.q: In which decade did the film Sankarabharanam, the celebrated Carnatic-music drama, premiere?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam was released in 1980, at the start of the 1980s.

questions.q: The lead actor of Sankarabharanam, J. V. Somayajulu, was relatively unknown in films before this role. What best describes his background?
options: He was a veteran action-film star
He came largely from a stage and theatre background
He was already a top playback singer
He was a famous classical dancer
questions.explanation: J. V. Somayajulu had a theatre background and shot to fame with his portrayal of Sankara Sastry.

questions.q: Which of these songs is a famous Carnatic-based number from the 1980 film Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: 'Omkaara Naadanu' is one of the celebrated classical songs from Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: The plot of the 1980 film Sankarabharanam explores the relationship between the vocalist Sankara Sastry and which other character?
options: A devoted young woman, Tulasi, and her son
questions.explanation: The film follows Sankara Sastry's bond with the devoted Tulasi and her son, who carries on his musical legacy.

questions.q: The 1980 film Sankarabharanam is widely seen as a turning point for which industry?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam is regarded as a landmark and turning point in Telugu cinema.

questions.q: A recurring conflict in the 1980 film Sankarabharanam is the tension between Carnatic classical music and what?
options: The rising popularity of Western and modern popular music
questions.explanation: The film dramatizes the decline of classical music amid the growing popularity of Western and modern styles.

questions.q: Beyond Telugu audiences, how did the 1980 film Sankarabharanam fare with viewers outside its home region?
options: It was never screened outside Andhra Pradesh
It was rejected everywhere outside the Telugu states
It found admiration across India despite the language barrier
It was remade before its original release elsewhere
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam won admirers across India, transcending its regional and linguistic origins.

questions.q: Who produced the 1980 film Sankarabharanam under the Poornodaya Movie Creations banner?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam was produced by Edida Nageswara Rao under the Poornodaya Movie Creations banner.

questions.q: At the National Film Awards, the 1980 film Sankarabharanam won Best Music Direction for K. V. Mahadevan. Which other major National Award did the film notably receive?
options: Best Male Playback Singer for S. P. Balasubrahmanyam
questions.explanation: S. P. Balasubrahmanyam won the National Film Award for Best Male Playback Singer for Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: Besides music awards, in which National Film Award category did the 1980 film Sankarabharanam also receive a top regional honour?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam was honoured as the Best Feature Film in Telugu at the National Film Awards for its year, reflecting its standing in Telugu cinema.

questions.q: The female playback singer of the 1980 film Sankarabharanam, who rendered its classical numbers, also won a National Film Award for the film. Who was she?
questions.explanation: S. Janaki won the National Film Award for Best Female Playback Singer for her classical renditions in Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: K. Viswanath, director of Sankarabharanam, later received which of India's highest film honours?
options: He received no national film honour
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was awarded the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2016 for his contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: The story of the 1980 film Sankarabharanam centres on the changing fortunes of its vocalist hero. What broadly happens to Sankara Sastry over the course of the film?
options: He experiences fame, then decline and hardship, while his art endures
He rises to riches and never faces hardship
He abandons music to become a politician
He emigrates and gives up Carnatic music
questions.explanation: The film charts Sankara Sastry's earlier glory, his decline into hardship, and the enduring legacy of his music through Tulasi and her son.

questions.q: Following the success of Sankarabharanam, K. Viswanath continued his classical-arts theme with which acclaimed 1983 musical film?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath followed Sankarabharanam with the dance-centred classic Saagara Sangamam in 1983.

questions.q: What is widely regarded as the cultural significance of the 1980 film Sankarabharanam in Indian cinema history?
options: It introduced colour to Telugu cinema
It was the first Indian film shot abroad
It demonstrated that a classical-music art film could be a mass commercial success and renewed interest in Carnatic music
It was India's first film with synchronized sound
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam proved a serious classical-music art film could achieve mass success, sparking renewed popular interest in Carnatic music.
